artifacts.summary: Validates the pre-existing handoff brief (00_session-context-and-handoff.md) against the live modules/home/email/{aerc,mbsync,mail-sync,notmuch}.nix and modules/home/services/mail-sync-timer.nix files; inventories ~16 Task-NN comment citations into 11 rationale clusters; recommends a Nix generator to de-duplicate querymap-gmail/querymap-logos and the repeated folders-exclude line; resolves all five holistic-reconsideration decision points with recommendations grounded in the locally-installed aerc 0.21.0 man pages (aerc-accounts(5), aerc-notmuch(5)) and task 112's prior source-level research; establishes a rendered-config byte-diff verification approach against a confirmed-green home-manager build baseline.

findings_summary.comment_consolidation: ~16 distinct Task-NN/Regression-fix citations across aerc.nix, clustering into 11 rationale groups (tab-switch, delete/archive hardening, mail-sync keybind history, Proposed-* wrapper routing, reply-close -c, Enter part-cycle, native send -a archive-on-reply, maildir-store/multi-file-strategy x2, folders-exclude x2, check-mail, INBOX/querymap scoping x2). Recommendation: consolidate into forward-looking prose per group, demote task numbers to trailing citations, do not delete the underlying rationale.
findings_summary.querymap_dedup: querymap-gmail/querymap-logos are structurally identical apart from the account folder prefix, the archive-folder name (All_Mail vs Archive), and Gmail's extra Spam line. Recommend a let-bound Nix generator function producing both from small per-account records; target byte-identical rendered output.
findings_summary.folders_exclude_vs_whitelist_vs_maildir_: KEEP folders-exclude blacklist. maildir-account-path was already evaluated and explicitly rejected by task 112's own research (both accounts intentionally share one maildir-store/notmuch source root); a folders whitelist has the identical cross-account-awareness requirement as the current blacklist and buys no clarity. De-duplicate the identical folders-exclude line between [gmail]/[logos] instead of changing the mechanism.
findings_summary.alternatives_text_plain_vs_html: KEEP text/plain,text/html (plaintext-first) as the global viewer default. Flipping it is a real behavior change (global, not per-message) with real downsides (w3m filter correctness, privacy/speed); the existing <Enter>/:next-part cycle already serves the html preference as a low-cost opt-in per message. Document as considered-and-rejected rather than silently flipped.
findings_summary.enter_binding: KEEP <Enter> = :next-part in [view]. No native part-select concept exists in aerc; <Space>/<C-d>/<C-u> already fully cover pager scrolling so nothing unique is lost. Trim the comment from 5 lines to ~2 while preserving the trade-off statement.
findings_summary.logos_check_mail: KEEP [logos] check-mail unwired. Both accounts already get periodic sync via the systemd mail-sync-timer (mail-sync both, every 15 min); check-mail-cmd is secondary/convenience only. Wiring logos now would add a second undifferentiated failure-surfacing case before task 114's known-vs-real-failure policy exists. Recommend documenting this as an explicit decision citing task 114, not a silent omission.
findings_summary.cross_file_consistency: Verified folder names, tag semantics, and the three mail-sync entry points (aerc $ keybind, check-mail-cmd, notmuch preNew hook) all converge correctly on the same flock-serialized mail-sync wrapper. No inconsistency found.
findings_summary.additional_risk_beyond_brief: Task 112's report (finding-7) documents an unresolved, source-grounded risk: aerc's act-dir multi-file-strategy resolves curDir from the currently-open tab name, which does not match for the INBOX querymap alias (only literal Gmail/Logos tab names resolve) -- multi-file archive from the INBOX tab may silently fail with 'refusing to act on multiple files'. This was never live-verified (task 112 Phase 4 PARTIAL/skipped). The refactor must carry this forward as an explicitly flagged open item, not silently resolve or mask it.

non_goals_confirmed: No behavior redesign -- all five reconsideration points resolved as KEEP with documented rationale
No home-manager switch or live-mail action performed
No implementation edits made to aerc.nix during this research pass

verification.baseline_build: home-manager build --flake .#benjamin ran during this research session and exited 0 (green baseline confirmed before any implementation work).
verification.recommended_verification_approach: Capture the five rendered files above from a green baseline build before editing; after the refactor, rebuild and diff each pair. A pure-consolidation refactor should produce zero diffs in binds.conf/accounts.conf/querymap-*; aerc.conf comment-only changes are not expected to alter its parsed form.
verification.read_only_safe: All research this session was read-only plus one home-manager build (build-only, no switch, no mail mutation).

next_steps: Create an implementation plan consolidating the 11 rationale clusters into forward-looking documentation, implementing the querymap and folders-exclude Nix generator for de-duplication, and recording each of the five reconsideration decisions (all KEEP, with explicit documented rationale) -- verified via the rendered-config byte-diff approach plus a green home-manager build.
open_questions: Exact Nix idiom preferred for the querymap/folders-exclude generator (inline let-binding vs. a small local function vs. lib.generators) -- left for the plan phase to decide within nix-style-guide.md conventions.

Whether to add a one-line pointer comment near multi-file-strategy flagging the unresolved task-112 finding-7 curDir/INBOX-tab risk, or leave it fully external to this file -- left for the plan phase.
